chapter_title.en: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions

text: : "لَيْسَ لِلْمَرْأَةِ مَعَ زَوْجِهَا أَمْرٌ فِي عِتْقٍ وَ لاَ صَدَقَةٍ وَ لاَ تَدْبِيرٍ وَ لاَ هِبَةٍ وَ لاَ نَذْرٍ فِي مَالِهَا إِلاَّ بِإِذْنِ زَوْجِهَا إِلاَّ فِي زَكَاةٍ أَوْ بِرِّ وَالِدَيْهَا أَوْ صِلَةِ قَرَابَتِهَا".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.3670 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said: "A woman has no authority with her husband regarding emancipation, charity, endowment, gift, or vow concerning her wealth, except with her husband's permission-except in the case of Zakat, kindness to her parents, or maintaining ties with her relatives."
